export default abstract class Server<
  ServerOptions extends Options = Options,
  ServerRequest extends BaseNextRequest = BaseNextRequest,
  ServerResponse extends BaseNextResponse = BaseNextResponse,
> {
  public readonly hostname?: string
  public readonly fetchHostname?: string
  public readonly port?: number
  protected readonly dir: string
  protected readonly quiet: boolean
  protected readonly nextConfig: NextConfigRuntime
  protected readonly distDir: string
  protected readonly publicDir: string
  protected readonly hasStaticDir: boolean
  protected readonly pagesManifest?: PagesManifest
  protected readonly appPathsManifest?: PagesManifest
  protected readonly buildId: string
  protected readonly deploymentId: string
  protected readonly dev: boolean
  protected readonly minimalMode: boolean
  protected readonly renderOpts: BaseRenderOpts
  protected readonly serverOptions: Readonly<ServerOptions>
  protected readonly appPathRoutes?: Record<string, string[]>
  protected readonly clientReferenceManifest?: DeepReadonly<ClientReferenceManifest>
  protected interceptionRoutePatterns: RegExp[]
  protected nextFontManifest?: DeepReadonly<NextFontManifest>
  protected instrumentation: InstrumentationModule | undefined

  protected abstract getPublicDir(): string
  protected abstract getHasStaticDir(): boolean
  protected abstract getPagesManifest(): PagesManifest | undefined
  protected abstract getAppPathsManifest(): PagesManifest | undefined
  protected abstract getBuildId(): string
  protected abstract getinterceptionRoutePatterns(): RegExp[]

  protected readonly enabledDirectories: NextEnabledDirectories
  protected abstract getEnabledDirectories(dev: boolean): NextEnabledDirectories

  protected readonly experimentalTestProxy?: boolean

  protected abstract findPageComponents(params: {
    locale: string | undefined
    page: string
    query: NextParsedUrlQuery
    params: Params
    isAppPath: boolean
    // The following parameters are used in the development server's
    // implementation.
    sriEnabled?: boolean
    appPaths?: ReadonlyArray<string> | null
    shouldEnsure?: boolean
    url?: string
  }): Promise<FindComponentsResult | null>
  protected abstract getPrerenderManifest(): DeepReadonly<PrerenderManifest>
  protected abstract getNextFontManifest():
    | DeepReadonly<NextFontManifest>
    | undefined
  protected abstract attachRequestMeta(
    req: ServerRequest,
    parsedUrl: NextUrlWithParsedQuery
  ): void
  protected abstract hasPage(pathname: string): Promise<boolean>

  protected abstract sendRenderResult(
    req: ServerRequest,
    res: ServerResponse,
    options: {
      result: RenderResult
      generateEtags: boolean
      poweredByHeader: boolean
      cacheControl: CacheControl | undefined
    }
  ): Promise<void>

  protected abstract runApi(
    req: ServerRequest,
    res: ServerResponse,
    query: ParsedUrlQuery,
    match: PagesAPIRouteMatch
  ): Promise<boolean>

  protected abstract renderHTML(
    req: ServerRequest,
    res: ServerResponse,
    pathname: string,
    query: NextParsedUrlQuery,
    renderOpts: LoadedRenderOpts
  ): Promise<RenderResult>

  protected abstract getIncrementalCache(options: {
    requestHeaders: Record<string, undefined | string | string[]>
  }): Promise<import('./lib/incremental-cache').IncrementalCache>

  protected getServerComponentsHmrCache():
    | ServerComponentsHmrCache
    | undefined {
    return this.nextConfig.experimental.serverComponentsHmrCache
      ? (globalThis as any).__serverComponentsHmrCache
      : undefined
  }

  protected abstract loadEnvConfig(params: {
    dev: boolean
    forceReload: boolean
  }): void

  // TODO-APP: (wyattjoh): Make protected again. Used for turbopack in route-resolver.ts right now.
  public readonly matchers: RouteMatcherManager
  protected readonly i18nProvider?: I18NProvider
  protected readonly localeNormalizer?: LocaleRouteNormalizer

  protected readonly normalizers: {
    readonly rsc: RSCPathnameNormalizer | undefined
    readonly segmentPrefetchRSC: SegmentPrefixRSCPathnameNormalizer | undefined
    readonly data: NextDataPathnameNormalizer | undefined
  }

  private readonly isAppPPREnabled: boolean

  /**

877	  public async handleRequest(
878	    req: ServerRequest,
879	    res: ServerResponse,
880	    parsedUrl?: NextUrlWithParsedQuery
881	  ): Promise<void> {
882	    await this.prepare()
883	    const method = req.method.toUpperCase()
884	    const tracer = getTracer()
885	
886	    return tracer.withPropagatedContext(req.headers, () => {
887	      // Capture the parent span before creating the handleRequest span.
888	      // When deployed with an adapter, the platform's runtime may create its
889	      // own OTEL HTTP server span before Next.js runs. We propagate http.route
890	      // to this parent span so APM tools (e.g. Datadog) can derive the
891	      // resource name correctly.
892	      const parentSpan = tracer.getActiveScopeSpan()
893	
894	      return tracer.trace(
895	        BaseServerSpan.handleRequest,
896	        {
897	          spanName: `${method}`,
898	          kind: SpanKind.SERVER,
899	          attributes: {
900	            'http.method': method,
901	            'http.target': req.url,
902	          },
903	        },
904	        async (span) =>
905	          this.handleRequestImpl(req, res, parsedUrl).finally(() => {
906	            if (!span) return
907	
908	            const isRSCRequest = getRequestMeta(req, 'isRSCRequest') ?? false
909	            span.setAttributes({
910	              'http.status_code': res.statusCode,
911	              'next.rsc': isRSCRequest,
912	            })
913	
914	            if (res.statusCode && res.statusCode >= 500) {
915	              // For 5xx status codes: SHOULD be set to 'Error' span status.
916	              // x-ref: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/http/http-spans/#status
917	              span.setStatus({
918	                code: SpanStatusCode.ERROR,
919	              })
920	              // For span status 'Error', SHOULD set 'error.type' attribute.
921	              span.setAttribute('error.type', res.statusCode.toString())
922	            }
923	
924	            const rootSpanAttributes = tracer.getRootSpanAttributes()

## Step 1: Server Architecture Orientation

The Next.js server has this class hierarchy:
- `Server` (abstract, `base-server.ts:316`) — The base class containing all routing, rendering orchestration, cache, SSG/ISR logic. Defines abstract methods for `renderHTML`, `findPageComponents`, `attachRequestMeta`, etc.


- `NextNodeServer extends Server` (`next-server.ts:175`) — Node.js production implementation that handles rendering through either the App Router or Pages Router, implements page component discovery, and attaches request metadata.
- `NextServer` (`next.ts:125`) — Thin wrapper/facade used by programmatic API users (`require('next')`)

The codebase is organized with the App Router rendering engine in `app-render/` and the route module system in `route-modules/`, where each route type has its own module implementation.

Pages Router uses a straightforward path through `renderHTML` → `lazyRenderPagesPage` → `renderToHTML`, while the App Router can take either a legacy path or go through the route module system with `renderToHTMLOrFlight`.

Now I'm looking at how the full SSR request lifecycle flows from the initial HTTP entry point. The render flow then moves into `renderToHTMLOrFlight`, which parses request headers and sets up a work store to manage the rendering context, before executing the actual render operation with async storage.

For pages using the older router, it takes a different path through `lazyRenderPagesPage` instead. The key entry points across these layers are spread through several files: `base-server.ts` handles the initial request routing and response orchestration, `next-server.ts` manages metadata attachment and component discovery, `load-components.ts` loads the compiled modules, and `app-render.tsx` orchestrates the actual rendering for app routes.

Now looking at who calls `renderToHTMLOrFlight` — it's invoked directly from `AppPageRouteModule.render` in the app-page route module, and also indirectly through `lazyRenderAppPage` which gets called from `NextNodeServer.renderHTMLImpl`. The function itself is exported from `app-render.tsx`.

For threading a request ID through the system, I need to figure out the best injection point. The key question is where to generate and pass this ID so it flows through all these call paths.

The cleanest place to generate it would be in `attachRequestMeta` early in the request lifecycle, before routing and rendering even start. For storage, I'm weighing two approaches: adding a `requestId` field directly to the existing `RequestMeta` interface since it already carries per-request context and flows through the entire pipeline, versus using AsyncLocalStorage which would require a different pattern.

RequestMeta is the better choice here — it's already the natural container for request-scoped data and threads through every layer from `handleRequestImpl` all the way down to rendering. The AsyncLocalStorage approach would be initialized too late in the App Router path and wouldn't cover Pages Router anyway.

So the plan is to generate the ID in `attachRequestMeta`, add the field to the `RequestMeta` interface, then set it as a response header early in the rendering pipeline.

But there's a wrinkle with `ResponseCache` and its `Batcher` — when multiple requests hit the same ISR cache key concurrently, only the first one that wins the batcher slot will set the correct response header. The subsequent coalesced requests get the same cached body but their response headers won't reflect their own request IDs.

The fix is to set the header outside the batcher callback, after the render completes...

## Step 4: Where to Thread a Request ID

### Where to generate the ID

**`NextNodeServer.attachRequestMeta`** (`next-server.ts:1954`) is the ideal generation site. It is called at `base-server.ts:1035` inside `handleRequestImpl` — before routing, before component loading, before any rendering. It already stamps `initURL`, `initQuery`, and `initProtocol` onto the request. A `crypto.randomUUID()` call here is zero-overhead and sets the ID at the earliest possible moment.

### Storage: RequestMeta is the right anchor

`RequestMeta` (`request-meta.ts:51`) is a typed interface stored at `Symbol.for('NextInternalRequestMeta')` on every `req` object. It flows through the entire pipeline already — `handleRequestImpl`, `renderToResponseWithComponentsImpl`, `renderHTML`, all the way to `renderToHTMLOrFlight` via the `req` argument. The infrastructure (`addRequestMeta`, `getRequestMeta`, `setRequestMeta`) is already in place.

Add one field:
```typescript
// request-meta.ts, inside RequestMeta interface
requestId?: string
```

Note: `NEXT_REQUEST_ID_HEADER = 'x-nextjs-request-id'` and `NEXT_HTML_REQUEST_ID_HEADER = 'x-nextjs-html-request-id'` are **already declared** in `packages/next/src/client/components/app-router-headers.ts:49-50` but have **zero callers** — the infrastructure was stubbed out and never wired up.

### Propagation strategy

**1. Generate** in `attachRequestMeta` (`next-server.ts:1954`):
```typescript
addRequestMeta(req, 'requestId', crypto.randomUUID())
```

**2. Set the response header** early in `renderToResponseWithComponentsImpl` (`base-server.ts:2026`), **before** the response-cache batcher is consulted — around line 2135 after `setVaryHeader` is called:
```typescript
const requestId = getRequestMeta(req, 'requestId')
if (requestId) res.setHeader(NEXT_REQUEST_ID_HEADER, requestId)
```

**3. Thread into `WorkStore`** for deep App Router access. `createWorkStore` is called at `app-render.tsx:3145`. Add `requestId` to `WorkStore` so Server Components can read it via `workAsyncStorage.getStore()?.requestId`. Pass it through `renderOpts` (already a catch-all options bag) or directly from `req`.

### Response-cache interaction — critical subtlety

`ResponseCache` (`response-cache/index.ts:107`) uses a `Batcher` that coalesces N concurrent requests for the same ISR cache key into a single render. Only the "winner" request's render callback runs; all others receive the same cached `RenderResult` body. If you set `X-Request-Id` inside the render callback, coalesced requests all get the winner's ID.

**Solution**: Set the header **on the individual `res` object before entering `responseCache.get()`**, using `getRequestMeta(req, 'requestId')`. The response body may be shared from cache, but each HTTP connection has its own `res` object, so each response gets a unique ID in its headers regardless.

## Step 4: Where to Thread a Request ID

### Where to generate the ID

**`NextNodeServer.attachRequestMeta`** (`next-server.ts:1954`) is the ideal generation site. It is called at `base-server.ts:1035` inside `handleRequestImpl` — before routing, before component loading, before any rendering. It already stamps `initURL`, `initQuery`, and `initProtocol` onto the request. A `crypto.randomUUID()` call here is zero-overhead and sets the ID at the earliest possible moment.

### Storage: RequestMeta is the right anchor

`RequestMeta` (`request-meta.ts:51`) is a typed interface stored at `Symbol.for('NextInternalRequestMeta')` on every `req` object. It flows through the entire pipeline already — `handleRequestImpl`, `renderToResponseWithComponentsImpl`, `renderHTML`, all the way to `renderToHTMLOrFlight` via the `req` argument. The infrastructure (`addRequestMeta`, `getRequestMeta`, `setRequestMeta`) is already in place.

Add one field:
```typescript
// request-meta.ts, inside RequestMeta interface
requestId?: string
```

Note: `NEXT_REQUEST_ID_HEADER = 'x-nextjs-request-id'` and `NEXT_HTML_REQUEST_ID_HEADER = 'x-nextjs-html-request-id'` are **already declared** in `packages/next/src/client/components/app-router-headers.ts:49-50` but have **zero callers** — the infrastructure was stubbed out and never wired up.

### Propagation strategy

**1. Generate** in `attachRequestMeta` (`next-server.ts:1954`):
```typescript
addRequestMeta(req, 'requestId', crypto.randomUUID())
```

**2. Set the response header** early in `renderToResponseWithComponentsImpl` (`base-server.ts:2026`), **before** the response-cache batcher is consulted — around line 2135 after `setVaryHeader` is called:
```typescript
const requestId = getRequestMeta(req, 'requestId')
if (requestId) res.setHeader(NEXT_REQUEST_ID_HEADER, requestId)
```

**3. Thread into `WorkStore`** for deep App Router access. `createWorkStore` is called at `app-render.tsx:3145`. Add `requestId` to `WorkStore` so Server Components can read it via `workAsyncStorage.getStore()?.requestId`. Pass it through `renderOpts` (already a catch-all options bag) or directly from `req`.

### Response-cache interaction — critical subtlety

`ResponseCache` (`response-cache/index.ts:107`) uses a `Batcher` that coalesces N concurrent requests for the same ISR cache key into a single render. Only the "winner" request's render callback runs; all others receive the same cached `RenderResult` body. If you set `X-Request-Id` inside the render callback, coalesced requests all get the winner's ID.

**Solution**: Set the header **on the individual `res` object before entering `responseCache.get()`**, using `getRequestMeta(req, 'requestId')`. The response body may be shared from cache, but each HTTP connection has its own `res` object, so each response gets a unique ID in its headers regardless.
